title: Welcome Lesson Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Hi! 👋 I'm Andrew. Welcome to **MaltiOnTheGo** — your free starter, survival Maltese for living and working in Malta.
If you've just landed and you've got a job to start, a flat to find, and a coffee to order — this lesson is for you. Four chunks. About three minutes. Let's go!
Here's what we'll cover: **polite essentials**, the **café** (because Malta runs on coffee ☕), surviving **at work**, and **shopping**. All everyday stuff.
Start with the magic three: hello, please, thanks. They open every door.
You'll hear this **constantly**. Use it from waking up until early afternoon.
After about 4pm. Notice how it sounds French — Maltese borrowed plenty from French and Italian. 🇫🇷
It's a long phrase but **slot it onto the end of any request** and you sound polite. Nixtieq kafè... jekk jogħġbok. 🎯
And the all-purpose response when someone apologises to you:
Goodbye options. Formal — wishes you health:
And to wish someone a good day:
Malta runs on coffee. You'll be ordering one every day. Memorise this section. ⚡
**'I would like'**. Just add what you want. ✅
And of course — the famous Maltese savoury pastry:
Then the question every café employee asks:
**How much does it cost?** Learn this one cold — you'll use it daily. 🎯
And when they ask if you want anything else:
Real talk — most workplaces in Malta speak English. **But** dropping a few Maltese phrases earns you respect, fast. 🤝
If someone speaks Maltese to you, you have a few escape hatches.
I'm learning Maltese — score points with this one:
And the lifesaver — do you speak English?
Almost everyone does — but it's polite to ask in Maltese first. ✨
The same magic phrase from the café works everywhere:
Quick tip — when prices come at you fast, ask them to write it down:
Could you write it? Sometimes easier than asking them to slow down. 🎯
Polite essentials. The café. At work. Shopping.
These four sections cover **80% of everyday interactions** as a working expat in Malta. Drill them, use them, watch how Maltese people light up when you try. 💪
Tap a section and start. Saħħa! 🇲🇹
